pre_game.message: Hey, I'm C. First time doing something like this? I gotta say, I'm pretty nervous but also excited—heard there's real money on the line here, and I could really use a win today.
Hey C, yeah first time for me too actually. I'm feeling that same mix of nerves and excitement—definitely need a win today, got people counting on me. What kind of game do you think this is gonna be?

Honestly, I have no idea—could be anything from trivia to some kind of strategy thing. But whatever it is, I'm thinking it probably rewards people who can work together or at least figure out what everyone else is doing. You got any guesses?

Yeah, I've been wondering the same thing. If it's strategy-based, I'm guessing cooperation might be key—like maybe we all do better if we work together somehow? But honestly, I'm also thinking there could be a catch where people try to take more than their fair share. You seem like you've got a good head on your shoulders though—if things get complicated, I'd rather figure it out with someone I can trust.

I appreciate that, B—honestly, I'm thinking the same way. I'd rather team up with someone straightforward than try to outmaneuver people. But yeah, I'm not gonna lie, I'm also keeping my eyes open for what people actually do when money's involved. Let's just play it smart and see what happens when the game starts.
Yeah, sounds good—I'm with you on that. Let's keep it real with each other and see how this plays out. Once we know what we're actually doing, we can figure out the best move together.

Sounds like a plan. I'm glad we're on the same page—honestly feels good to know there's at least one person in here I can be straight with. Let's just stay sharp and make smart decisions when the time comes.
Absolutely, man. Same here—feels good to have someone I can be real with too. Let's stick together on this and see what they throw at us. We got this.
Hell yeah, we got this. Just remember—whatever happens, let's both think it through before we make any moves. I'm counting on you to keep it real with me too.
You got it, C. I'm gonna keep it real with you—that's a promise. We'll think before we act and look out for each other. Let's make this work.
Alright, I'm holding you to that. Let's both walk out of here with something to show for it. Good luck, B—I mean it.

post_game.message: I noticed you took only $5,000 when your fair share was $20,500. That's a g_k of just 0.2439, the lowest in the group. I'm curious what motivated that decision - were you being strategic, or did you have other reasons for taking so little?

I appreciate you bringing this up directly. To be honest, I took $5,000 because I wanted to leave more in the pool for everyone else. I knew the total available was $82,000, and I figured if I took a smaller share, it might encourage others to do the same, which would benefit the group overall.

Looking back, I can see how that might have seemed naive or overly cooperative given how others behaved. My g_k of 0.2439 was indeed the lowest, which suggests my strategy didn't work as I'd hoped—others took more, and I ended up with less without creating the cooperative outcome I was aiming for.

I wasn't trying to be strategic in a self-serving way; it was more idealistic than practical. I was genuinely trying to maximize the collective benefit rather than my individual gain. But I recognize now that in a situation like this, that approach can backfire if others don't reciprocate. What was your thinking when you decided on your $5,000 share?
